focus: Sentence Stress & Rhythm Integration — Combining All L1 Connected-Speech Skills
focus_ar: دمج نبر الجملة والإيقاع — تجميع كل مهارات الكلام المتصل في المستوى الأول
arabic_challenge: By this week, you have learned five separate connected-speech skills: word stress (Wk1), flap T/D (Wk2), C-to-V linking (Wk3), glottal stop T (Wk4), V-to-V linking (Wk5), dropped H (Wk6), C-to-C linking (Wk7), and schwa (Wk8). The real challenge now is not learning something new, but making all of these run AUTOMATICALLY and SIMULTANEOUSLY in normal, unrehearsed speech — which is a different skill from executing each one in isolation.

target_sounds: integration: word stress + schwa reduction together

isolation.instructions: Practice sentences where correct word stress AND correct schwa reduction must happen together — if you get the stress right but pronounce the unstressed vowels fully, it still sounds foreign.
isolation.instructions_ar: تدرب على جمل لازم فيها النبر الصحيح وتخفيض الشوا الصحيح يحصلوا مع بعض — لو ضبطت النبر بس نطقت حروف العلة الباقية كاملة، برضه هيبان غريب.
practice: ə-BOUT the CAM-ər-ə (stress on caps, schwa elsewhere)

pair: AH-bout the CA-ME-RA (stress only, no schwa, still foreign)

sentence_practice: About the camera, the teacher is away again.
I forgot a family photo again.
The teacher is away, so I forgot about the camera.
daily_drills.record_this: About the camera — the teacher is away again, and I forgot a family photo, so I forgot about the whole thing.
daily_drills.self_check: Are BOTH your stressed syllables strong/clear AND your unstressed syllables reduced to schwa, in the same sentence?

target_sounds: integration: linking (all 3 types) + flap/glottal stop together

isolation.instructions: Practice sentences combining consonant-vowel linking, vowel-vowel linking, consonant-consonant linking, AND the flap/glottal-stop T patterns — all in the same breath.
isolation.instructions_ar: تدرب على جمل تجمع ربط الساكن بالمتحرك، والمتحرك بالمتحرك، والساكن بالساكن، مع أنماط الفلاب/الوقفة الحنجرية — كل ده في نفس الجملة.
practice: pick up the water bottle → linking + flap
she asked about the mountain → V-V linking + glottal stop
big city water → C-C linking + flap

pair: pick up the water bottle (all features combined)
pick. up. the. water. bottle. (choppy, no features)

sentence_practice: Pick up the water bottle near the big city fountain.
She asked about the mountain and the important button.
It's a big city, and the water is better there.
daily_drills.record_this: Pick up the water bottle near the big city fountain — she asked about the mountain, and it's an important, better place to visit.
daily_drills.self_check: Go phrase by phrase: did you correctly apply linking AND flap/glottal-stop in the same sentence without dropping one for the other?

target_sounds: integration: dropped H + linking + stress in natural dialogue

isolation.instructions: Simulate a short natural dialogue exchange, applying dropped H on pronouns, linking between words, and correct sentence stress all together.
isolation.instructions_ar: حاكي محادثة طبيعية قصيرة، وطبّق حذف H على الضمائر، والربط بين الكلمات، والنبر الصحيح للجملة كل ده مع بعض.
practice: Did you tell him? → linking + dropped H
I asked her about it → linking + dropped H + stress
He should call her again → stress + schwa + dropped H

pair: did you tell him about it (fully integrated)
did. you. tell. him. about. it. (choppy, no integration)

sentence_practice: Did you tell him about it? I asked her already.
He should call her again — did you ask him?
I asked her about it, but he should tell him first.
daily_drills.record_this: Did you tell him about it? I asked her already — he should call her again, but did you ask him first?
daily_drills.self_check: In this quick dialogue-style passage, count how many of your 8 learned features (stress, flap, glottal stop, 3 linking types, dropped H, schwa) appear naturally.

target_sounds: integration: full paragraph at moderate speed

isolation.instructions: Read a full short paragraph, deliberately checking for all 8 features as you go, at a comfortable but not slow pace.
isolation.instructions_ar: اقرأ فقرة قصيرة كاملة، وتأكد بشكل متعمد من كل الـ ٨ ميزات وانت بتقرأ، بسرعة مريحة بس مش بطيئة.
practice: Read the paragraph once slowly to plan each feature, then again at normal speed.

sentence_practice: About an hour ago, I picked up an apple and some water near the big game.
Did you tell him the button on the camera is broken?
It's a big deal — ask her about the mountain trip again.
daily_drills.record_this: About an hour ago, I picked up an apple and some water near the big game. Did you tell him the button on the camera is broken? It's a big deal — ask her about the mountain trip again.
daily_drills.self_check: Listen back once for EACH feature separately (8 total passes if needed) — is every feature present at least once in this short passage?

isolation.instructions: Today, do NOT read from a script. Speak spontaneously for 30-45 seconds about your day, then listen back and identify which of the 8 features appeared naturally without you planning them.
isolation.instructions_ar: النهارده متقراش من نص. اتكلم بشكل عفوي لمدة ٣٠-٤٥ ثانية عن يومك، وبعدين استمع وحدد أي من الـ ٨ ميزات ظهرت طبيعي بدون تخطيط.
practice: Talk about: what you did today, your plans for tomorrow, or a recent conversation with a friend.

word_practice: (free choice — use your own vocabulary from this week's theme)
sentence_practice: (No fixed sentences — this is a free-speech day.)
daily_drills.record_this: (Free speech: talk for 30-45 seconds about your day without a script.)
daily_drills.self_check: Which of the 8 features appeared WITHOUT you consciously trying? Those are becoming automatic. Which ones didn't appear at all? Those need more Week 1-8 review.

daily_drills.target_sounds: Review: full integration of stress, flap, glottal stop, 3 linking types, dropped H, and schwa
challenge_sentences: About an hour ago, my big brother asked her about the mountain trip — did you tell him the button on the camera broke?
Pick up the water bottle, ask about the big game, and tell him it's an important, better deal.
She should call him again — it doesn't matter, the city water tastes better than the mountain's.
daily_drills.record_this: About an hour ago, my big brother asked her about the mountain trip — did you tell him the camera's button broke? Pick up the water, ask about the big game, and tell him it's a better, more important deal than the mountain's water.
daily_drills.self_check: This is your most demanding integration passage yet. Go through it feature by feature (all 8) and self-score honestly using the L0 Day-7 style scoring guide.

daily_drills.target_sounds: Self-Assessment + Extended Practice — Full L1 Accent Integration Check

test_yourself.instructions: Record 60-90 seconds of natural, semi-spontaneous speech about a topic of your choice (your week, a goal, a memory). This is a comprehensive checkpoint before Week 10's final review and the L0→L1 advancement exam's accent section.
test_yourself.passage: (Semi-spontaneous — choose your own topic, but aim to naturally include: at least 3 stressed content words with reduced function words around them, at least 2 flap-T or glottal-stop-T words, at least 2 examples of any linking type, at least 1 dropped-H pronoun, and at least 3 schwa syllables.)

scoring_guide.90_100: All 8 features present and natural-sounding in spontaneous speech, without conscious effort.
scoring_guide.70_89: Most features present; 1-2 need conscious effort rather than being automatic yet.
scoring_guide.50_69: Features present only when reading a script, absent in spontaneous speech.
scoring_guide.below_50: Return to Weeks 1-8 and repeat the weakest 2-3 features individually before continuing.

for_arabic_speakers: This week is intentionally a synthesis week, not a new-content week — real fluency comes from combining small skills, not from learning more new rules.
It's normal for integration to feel harder than any single week's drills — running 8 systems at once IS harder than running one at a time. This is expected, not a sign of failure.
Use Day 5's spontaneous speech recording as your honest baseline — scripted reading always looks better than free speech at this stage, and that gap will close with more real conversation practice.
supplementary_notes.daily_time: 20-25 minutes this week (longer due to integration practice and free-speech recording)
supplementary_notes.recording_requirement: Record Day 5's spontaneous speech AND Day 7's assessment — compare them to see how much more natural free speech becomes with conscious practice.
